description: In March 2018 I dive into a new adventure at THEOplayer. I start out as a Full Stack Developer where I work on new services and tools to improve and enhance the THEOplayer experience.
After a few months I grow into the role of Team Lead and build out a team to further improve and build these experiences.
I also shift my technical focus. I dive into the world of JavaScript and NodeJS with a big focus on TypeScript, React and GraphQL!
In November 2015 I join Boondoggle, I get to start on a new team that builds out one largest projects within the company

As part of a larger consortium our team builds out the frontend tier for Argenta Netherlands, a large bank in the Netherlands. We build out the basis for a new online banking platform within a year. The next year we continue adding additional features and extra services to enhance the Argenta online banking experience.

In this large project most of my focus goes to integrating a collection of different backends and combine them into an easy to consume API for the frontend application powered by Sitecore and Angular.
I also become a Sitecore Professional Developer by obtaining my Sitecore Certification
In October 2012 I join SBB Accountants & Adviseurs. As a junior I get my first experience as a .NET developer.

I end up becoming the lead developer of the project in charge of rewriting their core in-house invoicing system which is used to bill over 10 000 active customers on a monthly basis for various accounting services.
By the end of 2014, I am responsbile for developing the part in charge of the direct debits and build a SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area) complient direct debit system.
In April 2012 I get my first professional experience in the field thanks to a great internship at Carabas.
I get to work as a PHP developer to help and maintain their in-house Content Management System and e-mail marketing platform.
In Februari 2012 I get to work on an online platform for managing different organisations who support and aid in the education offered by the SKBL-school-community.
The platform, as graduation project together with 3 other students, uses technologies like ASP.NET MVC and Entity Framework
